items.title: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5 billion commitment
items.raw_content: Microsoft follows Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic with its new AI deployment group.
items.summary: Microsoft follows Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic with its new AI deployment group.

items.title: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung
items.raw_content: The news comes about a week after OpenAI announced its own custom AI chip in a partnership with Broadcom.
items.summary: The news comes about a week after OpenAI announced its own custom AI chip in a partnership with Broadcom.

items.title: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
items.raw_content: Meta has quietly launched Pocket, an experimental AI app that lets users generate and share interactive mini games using text prompts.
items.summary: Meta has quietly launched Pocket, an experimental AI app that lets users generate and share interactive mini games using text prompts.

items.title: Yep, we’re using OpenClaw to date now
items.raw_content: Ben Guez has "a bunch of potential international wives in [his] DMs," thanks to an automated script he set up using OpenClaw, Claude code, and Instagram trials.
items.summary: Ben Guez has "a bunch of potential international wives in [his] DMs," thanks to an automated script he set up using OpenClaw, Claude code, and Instagram trials.

items.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
items.raw_content: At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.
items.summary: At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.

items.title: OpenAI proposed donating 5% of its equity to a US sovereign wealth fund
items.raw_content: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reportedly proposed giving 5% of the company’s equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, reviving discussions about letting the public share in the financial gains from the AI boom.
items.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reportedly proposed giving 5% of the company’s equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, reviving discussions about letting the public share in the financial gains from the AI

items.title: OpenAI floats giving Trump administration 5 percent cut of AI boom
items.raw_content: OpenAI has floated giving the US government a 5 percent ownership stake as a way of easing tensions with the Trump administration and blunting mounting public backlash against AI, according to the Financial Times. CEO Sam Altman argued that giving the public a financial interest in the company would be the best way to share […]

items.title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become
items.raw_content: Just for kicks, I took a look at Jersey Mike's IPO documents. Surely a sandwich shop would have no need to mention AI. But lo and behold.

items.title: Anthropic’s long-sidelined Fable 5 is greenlit to return
items.raw_content: After weeks of negotiating with the Trump administration, Anthropic is finally going to be able to bring Claude Fable 5 back online. In a post on X, Anthropic said it plans to begin restoring access Wednesday to users globally on Claude platforms, and that the company would re-enable access on AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft […]

items.title: Ashton Kutcher leaving Sound Ventures to launch new VC firm with Morgan Beller
items.raw_content: Sound built its reputation on concentrated, high-conviction bets in category-leading AI labs, while Kutcher's new fund appears to be chasing the layer underneath those companies — the infrastructure and energy that power them.

items.title: Google built a great smart speaker, but Gemini isn’t ready for it
items.raw_content: Smart speakers have spent the past few years searching for a compelling second act. Beyond music, timers, and controlling your lights, they've struggled to justify taking up space on the kitchen counter. AI promised to change that. Amazon debuted its new hardware powered by a revamped Alexa last fall, and now it's finally Google's turn. […]

items.title: Lawmakers want to ban AI companies from selling your health data
items.raw_content: A new proposal would ban the sale of Americans' health and location information to data brokers - including information people reveal to an AI chatbot like ChatGPT or Claude. In the coming weeks, Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) and Representative Mary Gay Scanlon (D-PA) are planning to debut a new version of the Health and Location […]

items.title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30M of his own money to build AI alternative to Microsoft Office
items.raw_content: Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office and Google Apps with AI.
items.summary: Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office and Google Apps with AI.

items.title: SpaceX has an AI device prototype, and it sure sounds phone-ish
items.raw_content: SpaceX reportedly showed investors a "handset-like" AI device before going public. It could be another signal SpaceX wants to expand into wireless.
items.summary: SpaceX reportedly showed investors a "handset-like" AI device before going public. It could be another signal SpaceX wants to expand into wireless.

items.title: Google’s NotebookLM can sum up your research in a TikTok-style clip
items.raw_content: Google's NotebookLM is adding a new way to catch up on your notes: TikTok-style AI videos. The new feature is rolling out to Google AI Ultra and Pro subscribers, allowing NotebookLM to generate 60-second vertical AI clips based on the sources you upload to the app. The example shared by Google details Australia's unsuccessful war […]

items.title: Netflix is using an AI-generated Gene Wilder voice in its Willy Wonka reality show
items.raw_content: A new teaser trailer confirmed that Wonka's The Golden Ticket will premiere on Netflix on September 23rd, following its Squid Game reality show in the trend of creating real competitions based on fictional torture scenarios. items.title: OpenAI is teasing new hardware… for Codex
items.raw_content: OpenAI is releasing some sort of device related to its AI-powered coding tool, Codex, on July 15th. items.title: Tidal won’t pay royalties on AI-generated music, but isn’t banning it outright
items.raw_content: Tidal shared its new policies regarding AI-generated music today and how the platform plans to "protect artists" and "inform listeners." Instead of banning it outright, starting on July 15th Tidal will label tracks it has identified as being 100 percent AI-generated with an icon. But starting today those tracks will no longer be monetizable. "Tidal's […]
